en.extract:
The hook-billed bulbul (Setornis criniger) is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in eastern Sumatra and Borneo, where its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical swamps. It is threatened by habitat loss. Alternate names for the hook-billed bulbul include the long hook-billed bulbul and long-billed bulbul.
While currently monotypic within the genus Setornis, originally the genus contained the hairy-backed bulbul (now Tricholestes criniger viridis).


References

The '''hook-billed bulbul''' ('''''Setornis criniger''''') is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in eastern [[Sumatra]] and [[Borneo]], where its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] [[swamp]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name="iucn status 17 November 2021" /> Alternate names for the hook-billed bulbul include the '''long hook-billed bulbul''' and '''long-billed bulbul'''.

While currently [[monotypic]] within the genus '''''Setornis''''',<ref name = "ITIS"/> originally the genus contained the [[hairy-backed bulbul]] (now ''Tricholestes criniger viridis'').<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=746153FE&sec=summary&ssver=1|title=Tricholestes criniger viridis - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2017-06-03}}</ref>
